content.problemStatement:
Chef bought NNN different snacks, and now needs to decide which ones to eat.

The iii-th snack has a tastiness of AiA_iAi‚Äã, and contains BiB_iBi‚Äã units of sugar.

Note that AiA_iAi‚Äã can be 000 or even negative.

Chef wants to limit his sugar intake, so he will choose some **non-negative integer** LLL, and then he can only eat a snack if it has at most LLL units of sugar, i.e. that satisfies Bi‚â§LB_i \le LBi‚Äã‚â§L.

Note that he doesn't need to eat every snack that satisfies Bi‚â§LB_i \leq LBi‚Äã‚â§L: he can decide to skip some of them too.

Let SSS denote the sum of tastiness values of all the snacks Chef decides to eat, after choosing the value of LLL.

Chef's satisfaction then equals (S‚àíL)(S - L)(S‚àíL). That is, Chef's satisfaction is the sum of tastiness values of everything he eats, minus the threshold value LLL.

What is Chef's maximum possible satisfaction, if he chooses the value of LLL and the snacks he eats appropriately?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the number of snacks.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã ‚Äî the tastiness values.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã ‚Äî the sugar quantities.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum possible satisfaction Chef can achieve.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Choose L=2L = 2L=2, and eat only the first snack.
This gives a tastiness of A1=4A_1 = 4A1‚Äã=4, so the overall satisfaction is 4‚àí2=24 - 2 = 24‚àí2=2. This is optimal.
Test case 222: Choose L=4L = 4L=4, and eat snacks 1,2,41, 2, 41,2,4. This gives a satisfaction of (A1+A2+A4‚àíL)=(5+12+1‚àí4)=14(A_1 + A_2 + A_4 - L) = (5+12+1-4) = 14(A1‚Äã+A2‚Äã+A4‚Äã‚àíL)=(5+12+1‚àí4)=14, which is optimal.
Note that even though A3‚â§L=4A_3 \leq L = 4A3‚Äã‚â§L=4, it's ideal to not eat it.
Test case 333: Choose L=0L = 0L=0 and don't eat any snacks. The overall satisfaction is 000, which is still better than eating any snack.
